mcqs.question: In thread scheduling, which state indicates a thread is waiting for CPU allocation?
mcqs.explanation: The READY state means the thread is waiting in the ready queue for CPU allocation. A thread in RUNNING state is currently executing, BLOCKED threads are waiting for I/O or events, and TERMINATED threads have finished execution.

mcqs.question: Which scheduling algorithm provides the minimum average waiting time?
mcqs.explanation: SJF (Shortest Job First) provides minimum average waiting time among common algorithms because it always executes the shortest job first. However, it requires knowledge of burst times.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of the many-to-one threading model?

options.text: If one thread blocks, all threads in process block

mcqs.explanation: In many-to-one model, all user threads map to single kernel thread. When that kernel thread blocks on I/O, the entire process blocks because no other user thread can run.

mcqs.question: Context switching involves:

options.text: Saving and loading complete thread state

mcqs.explanation: Context switching saves the complete state of outgoing thread (registers, program counter, stack pointer, CPU flags) and loads state of incoming thread. This overhead affects system performance.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a scheduling criterion?
mcqs.explanation: Memory Access Time is a hardware characteristic, not a scheduling criterion. The five main criteria are CPU Utilization, Throughput, Turnaround Time, Waiting Time, and Response Time.

mcqs.question: In Round-Robin scheduling, if time quantum is too small, what happens?
mcqs.explanation: Very small time quantum causes frequent context switches as threads constantly get preempted. This increases overhead and reduces effective CPU time available for actual computation.

mcqs.question: What problem can occur in priority scheduling?
mcqs.explanation: Starvation (or priority inversion) occurs when high-priority threads continuously arrive, causing low-priority threads to wait indefinitely. Solution includes aging - gradually increasing priority of waiting threads.

mcqs.question: Which thread scheduling model provides maximum parallelism on multi-core systems?
mcqs.explanation: One-to-one model provides maximum parallelism because each user thread maps to its own kernel thread, allowing true concurrent execution on multiple CPU cores.

mcqs.question: Thread affinity is primarily used to:

options.text: Improve cache performance by keeping thread on same core

mcqs.explanation: Thread affinity binds threads to specific CPU cores to improve performance by maximizing cache locality. Keeping a thread on the same core preserves cached data.

mcqs.question: A thread pool helps in:

options.text: Reducing overhead of creating new threads for each task

mcqs.explanation: Thread pools reduce overhead by reusing existing threads instead of creating new ones for each task. This is especially beneficial in server applications handling many short-lived tasks.

mcqs.question: In FCFS scheduling, what is the convoy effect?

options.text: Short threads waiting for long threads to complete

mcqs.explanation: Convoy effect occurs when a long-running thread (like I/O-bound) holds CPU while short threads wait behind it, causing low CPU utilization and poor performance for interactive tasks.

mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of preemptive scheduling?
mcqs.explanation: Preemptive scheduling allows the OS to interrupt long-running threads to give CPU time to interactive/real-time threads, improving response time and system responsiveness.

options.text: Better response time for interactive threads

mcqs.question: Ready queue in thread scheduling contains threads in which state?
mcqs.explanation: The ready queue contains only threads in READY state - those that are created but waiting for CPU allocation. Running threads are on CPU, blocked threads in wait queues.

mcqs.question: Which metric measures how many threads complete per unit time?
mcqs.explanation: Throughput measures system productivity - number of threads/processes completed per time unit. Higher throughput indicates better system efficiency.

mcqs.question: In a multithreaded application on a single-core CPU, true parallelism is:

options.text: Not achievable - only one thread runs at a time

mcqs.explanation: On single-core CPU, only one thread executes at any instant despite multithreading. Concurrency is achieved through time-slicing (rapid switching), not true parallelism.

mcqs.question: What is the main goal of real-time thread scheduling?
mcqs.explanation: Real-time scheduling must guarantee that threads complete within specified deadlines. Missing deadlines in real-time systems can cause critical failures.

mcqs.question: Time quantum in Round-Robin scheduling should typically be:

options.text: Small enough to allow frequent switching but large enough to reduce overhead
Equal to burst time of shortest thread

mcqs.explanation: Optimal time quantum balances responsiveness and overhead. Too small causes excessive context switches, too large degrades to FCFS. Typical values are 10-100 milliseconds.

mcqs.question: Which scheduling algorithm may cause starvation?
mcqs.explanation: Priority scheduling without aging can cause starvation because low-priority threads may never get CPU if high-priority threads keep arriving. Round-Robin and FCFS are starvation-free.

mcqs.question: Turnaround time is calculated as:
mcqs.explanation: Turnaround Time = Completion Time - Arrival Time. It includes all phases: waiting in ready queue, execution time, and time in other states like I/O wait.

mcqs.question: Thread synchronization is related to scheduling because:

options.text: It determines which thread runs next
Improper synchronization can cause threads to block unnecessarily affecting scheduling efficiency
It has no relation to scheduling

mcqs.explanation: Thread synchronization (mutexes, semaphores) affects scheduling when threads block on locks. Poor synchronization can cause unnecessary blocking, reducing CPU utilization and affecting overall scheduling performance.
